rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBYN CHRISTINA LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Each petition must stand on its own and not make references or "augmentations" to prior petitions that have already been adjudicated. This third report did not appear to fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. (No courtesy copy was provided, so it had to be reviewed online.) A petition for final distribution or a fourth report of status must be filed at least two weeks before 9/30/24. Appearance will be required on 9/30/24 if no petition is timely filed. The next petition must include an accounting for the first two-years unless all interested parties waive an accounting by the administrator. [LRSF 14.34 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/16/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The supplement filed on 7/29/24 AMENDED the second amended petition prayer. [CRC 7.3.] The corrections were not merely typographical; they were substantive. An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) In both the second amended petition and in the supplement to the second amended petition, the net distributable amount to each heir (or successor to the heir) appears to be incorrect if cash on hand totaled $875,638.59. The administrator should show how the net amount was calculated. (3) The summary of account shall be supported by detailed schedules showing the following: (a) Receipts, showing the nature or purpose of each item, the source of the receipt, and the DATE thereof. [Probate Code Section 1062(a).] Receipts not properly accounted for will not be included in the statutory fee calculation. (The DETAILED gains on sale schedule should also show the DATE, the appraised value, and the sale price, not just the gain amount.) (4) The second amended petition does not address why the administrator is seeking $17,610.84 reimbursement of costs. The attorney invoices attached to the supplement filed on 7/29/24 do not show any description of the legal services. (5) The seller's closing statement showed $171.75 as the notary fee, not $300. (6) The verification of the second amended petition and the supplement to the second amended petition referred to the incorrect title of the second amended petition. (7) The notice of hearing also showed the incorrect caption of the second amended petition. Deadline is 9/6/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any responsive papers and a revised proposed order. An amendment to the second amended petition to approve final report, etc., must be filed to address the issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUM CHUEN TO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/24. (No earlier availability.) 1. An amended petition was filed on 6/24/24. No proof of publication of the amended petition has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] 2. The proof of service filed on 6/24/24 does not clearly indicate that an AMENDED petition will be heard. 3. The notice also indicates that the petition requests that a will be admitted and does not indicate that IAEA authority is being requested. Furthermore, filing a pleading on the date that the hearing will be heard (6/24/24) makes no sense. 4. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. (Please review the Court's website for the most recent information regarding remote appearances.) 5. If Decedent's half-sister Yuk Ying To died in 2018, and sister Kwai King To died in 2021, they POST-deceased. Who are their successors-in-interest? Their issue are not necessarily their successor(s)-in-interest. [LRSF 14.18 D.3.] Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY TINYEAH FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just filed on 8/2/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The notice of hearing did not show the full caption of the amended petition in item 1 and a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely was not attached. (3) Proposed distribution must show the specific assets to be distributed, including the net amount of cash and complete description of each individual security, not just the account number of the decedent's brokerage account. [CRC 7.651.] (4) All assets were reportedly held in "interest bearing accounts" but assets on hand are claimed to be the exact same amount as on the date of death. (5) Personal property taxes were paid...? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The executor has apparently NOT actually marshaled the estate assets, as he proposes to distribute the decedent's account, not the estate account, to himself as the trustee. Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: BELLAMY D. ACKRELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Approving Transfer Of Life Insurance Proceeds To Custodian Under California Uniform Transfers To Minors Act
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) This Court does not favor transfers to a CUTMA account of any funds payable to a minor. [LRSF 14.35 E.] (2) Please address more accurately how Probate Code Section 3900 et seq. provides authority for the relief sought, including extending the time for distribution to age 21. Unless the petition is withdrawn earlier, please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

(Item 7 of a petition for probate or for letters of administration should not be completed when issue survived.)
